zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Agnes Byres Stewart於1407年4月出生在蘇格蘭阿吉爾時，她的父親Robert Stewart First Lord of Lorn of Innermeath28歲，母親Johanna Margaret Joan Stewart32歲。她於1425年與John Lindsay - First Lord of the Byres在蘇格蘭哈丁頓史爾哈丁頓結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和10個女兒的父母。她去世於1477年在英國蘇格蘭易亞斯特 洛迪安享年70歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭藍納可爾克勞福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Agnes Byres Stewart于1407年4月出生在苏格兰阿吉爾时，她的父亲Robert Stewart First Lord of Lorn of Innermeath28岁，母亲Johanna Margaret Joan Stewart32岁。她于1425年与John Lindsay - First Lord of the Byres在苏格兰哈丁頓史爾哈丁頓结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和10个女儿的父母。她去世于1477年在英国苏格兰易亞斯特 洛迪安享年70岁，埋葬在苏格兰藍納可爾克勞福德。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

nameMeanings.name: Byres
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Byass
meanings: 1 English: locative name perhaps, as Redmonds suggests, a spelling of Byers, a reduced form of Birsey, from Bursea (ER Yorks).

2 English: relationship name perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English personal name * Bayus, a pet form of Old English female names beginning in Bēag- or Bēah- ‘ring’, such as Bēagswīð and Bēahhild, + the hypocoristic suffix -us.
3 English: locative name perhaps from Middle English bye hus ‘house on a corner’ (Old English byge ‘curve, bend, corner’ + hūs).

variants.name: Byers
meanings: Scottish (Borders) and English (Cumberland and Durham): topographic name for someone who lived by a cattleshed, Middle English byre, or a habitational name from any of several places called with Old English b̄re ‘cattleshed’, for example Byers Green in County Durham or Byres near Edinburgh.
English: variant of Byer , with either an original plural or a post-medieval excrescent -s.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Agnes Byres Stewart was born in April 1407, in Argyll, Scotland, her father, Robert Stewart First Lord of Lorn of Innermeath, was 28 and her mother, Johanna Margaret Joan Stewart, was 32. She married John Lindsay - First Lord of the Byres in 1425, in Haddington, Haddingtonshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 10 daughters. She died in 1477, in East Lothian, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 70, and was buried in Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Agnes Byres Stewart est née en avril 1407, à Argyll, Écosse, son père, Robert Stewart First Lord of Lorn of Innermeath, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Johanna Margaret Joan Stewart, avait 32 ans. Elle a épousé John Lindsay - First Lord of the Byres en 1425, à Haddington, Haddingtonshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 10 filles. Elle est décédée en 1477, à East Lothian, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterrée à Crawford, Lanarkshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Agnes Byres Stewart im April 1407 in Argyll, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert Stewart First Lord of Lorn of Innermeath 28 und ihre Mutter Johanna Margaret Joan Stewart 32. Sie hat 1425 in Haddington, Haddingtonshire, Scotland John Lindsay - First Lord of the Byres geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 10 Töchter. Sie starb 1477 im Alter von 70 Jahren in East Lothian, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Agnes Byres Stewart è nata il nel aprile 1407 nel Argyll, Scotland, sua padre, Robert Stewart First Lord of Lorn of Innermeath, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Johanna Margaret Joan Stewart, aveva 32 anni. Lei sposato John Lindsay - First Lord of the Byres nel 1425, nel Haddington, Haddingtonshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 10 figlie. Morì nel 1477, nel East Lothian, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 70 anni, e fu sepolta a Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Agnes Byres Stewartが1407年4月にスコットランドアルグイルで生まれた時，父Robert Stewart First Lord of Lorn of Innermeathは28歳，母Johanna Margaret Joan Stewartは32歳であった。1425年にスコットランドハッドイントンシュアイアハッドイントンでJohn Lindsay - First Lord of the Byresと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と10人の娘の親であった。彼女は1477年にイギリススコットランドエアスト ロスアンで70歳で死去し，スコットランドラナルクシュアイアクラウフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Agnes Byres Stewart(이)가 1407년 4월 스컷랜드 아그이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Stewart First Lord of Lorn of Innermeath(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Johanna Margaret Joan Stewart(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 1425년 스컷랜드 하딩토실르 하딩턴에서 John Lindsay - First Lord of the Byres(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 열 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1477년 영국 스코틀랜드 이스트 로스이언에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 크롤퍼드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Agnes Byres Stewart nasceu em abril de 1407, em Argyll, Scotland, seu pai, Robert Stewart First Lord of Lorn of Innermeath, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Johanna Margaret Joan Stewart, tinha 32 anos. Ela casou-se com John Lindsay - First Lord of the Byres em 1425, em Haddington, Haddingtonshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 10 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1477, em East Lothian, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 70 anos, e foi sepultada em Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Agnes Byres Stewart родилась апрель 1407 г. в местности аргилл, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Robert Stewart First Lord of Lorn of Innermeath было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Johanna Margaret Joan Stewart, 32. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Lindsay - First Lord of the Byres в 1425 г. в местности хаддингтон, хаддингтоншир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 10 дочерей. Она умерла в 1477 г. в местности эаст лотян, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 70 лет и была похоронена в местности кравфорд, ланаркшир, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Agnes Byres Stewart nació en abril de 1407, en Argyll, Scotland, su papá, Robert Stewart First Lord of Lorn of Innermeath, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Johanna Margaret Joan Stewart, tenía 32 años. Se casó con John Lindsay - First Lord of the Byres en 1425, en Haddington, Haddingtonshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 10 hijas. Murió en 1477, en East Lothian, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultada en Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland.
